item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …what where the [REDACTED] in agent █████,s sock drawer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for

SCP-1940-1 can be harmed, but doing so will cause another instance of SCP-1940-1 to materialize. SCP-1940-1 will continue to materialize indefinitely, though it will begin to beg and plead with its attacker.

The fact that this was undoubtedly discovered via repeated experimentation makes me chuckle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a bit silly and charming, but it's neither silly nor charming enough to make up for the fact that there's not much there there.

It's a shop that pops up anywhere, and will try to sell the stuff in that spot. There are some nods towards LCE stuff (I remember them, but not enough that it significantly piqued my interest) hinting at extra-dimensional weirdness, but you never seem to develop it. As-is, it just left me with a "yeah, and…?" feeling. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Light courier enterprises feels like a less evil version of the Factory
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the object in the interview sometimes SCP-1940-1 and sometimes SCP-1940-2?

item.title: Tag addition notice
item.content:encoded: Added the contagion tag, as the sold items spread the existence of 1940 to other rooms, conditionally. If this is considered too much of a stretch of the tag's definition (given how technically these only summon 1940 rather than directly cause it), feel free to remove it.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I cleaned up the writing and the tone, and made the interview a bit more consistent with what Light Courier Enterprises actually is. Nothing new for those who have read this already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. I can just imagine people being very confused when they go to retrieve something from… anything, like a tiny man trying to sell their jewelry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I'm still giggling from the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I pictured Zacherie from OFF, complete with his Judge mask and French accent…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An instance of SCP-1940 was found to have developed in Agent █████'s sock drawer.

Aahahahah!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it should be safe, since all you have to do is find any outside instances and neutralize them by buying their products and disposing of them to prevent any spreading. Unless you're considering -1 to be unpredictable, I might need a bit more convincing.
As for the dialogue, I'll try make it flow better.

Changed, since it's kinda uncontained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely improved, but you still have a few sticking points for me. IMO this should be a Euclid, given its properties, and the interview's dialogue is a little abrupt/not very realistic. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They destroy any products that spread the SCP, and -2 won't do anything if kept outdoors.

And about your second idea: I wasn't sure how to go about in explaining the origins of -1. Implementing this suggestion would take a lot of time for me, but I'll try and see if I can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just boring to me. It doesn't really go anywhere. No vote, but leaning on downvote. Also, is there any restriction to what rooms it manifests in? It seems like effective containment would be impossible.

When I was reading this, I kept thinking about how cool it would've been if the -1s had been sent here by some extradimensional corporation, and they were being extorted to work on Earth. The hook is that the Foundation had set up a big humanitarian aid program where we saved the -1s from SCP-1940, amnestic'd them, and sent them into our society to live free.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the previous contents of the room are for sale, not the cashier or cash register, which are part of the phenomenon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how this scip has developed so +1. I do have one question, if everything is for sale then what happens if you try to buy SCP-1940-1 or their cash register?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… the dialog feels kinda off in this one, and it doesn't add much. Kinda stretches the joke. I'd recommend working on tightening the description instead of adding on more stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: Added a new interview log…
I have a feeling it isn't necessary, but I would like some more opinions on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there is still definitely room for improvement, but this is improved enough from the draft you showed me that I can upvote it. I'd encourage you to keep going on and using the feedback you get from commentator to make it even better than it already is.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Don't respond to staff posts if you are not a staff member, it's against the site rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can definitely see the improvement here, but I still don't find this attention-grabbing enough.
+1 for now, hoping to see further improvement.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I'm always wary about changing typos. I'm sometimes prone to edit too much. Thanks for the reminder, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…the first typo was never there.
Otherwise, fixed.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I'd just like to remind everyone that, if you can make a post on the thread to complain about typos, you most likely have the edit privileges to fix those typos. Just leave a short description of the changes in the Edit Note space so the author knows what you did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I did intend this to be a cellular sort of organism that reproduced by spreading its products. I still consider it as exactly that.

Fuck no, past me. That's not what this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fix more typos, lose my downvote
– hide block

-was restrained following a violet outburst

- [due would look less internet-y as [Due

- [5 second pause]… Choose the pause or the ellipsis, you can't have both.

- if your unconvinced. you're

But do they accept Bitcoins???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I'm personally not really affected either way. Just curious, because that just struck me as something an organism or entity might have as an aspect of itself to make sure it doesn't die out. But that's probably just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the original SCP I wrote was spreadable, but when I think about it in it's current state, I think that it would cause the development of the SCP in many different "rooms", such as a briefcase. I find thinking of that happening as hilarious, though I'm not sure if it takes away from this as a whole. Unless you think it does, I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. I'm not feeling this, maybe because I've done an idea that is very, very loosely similar to this one.

One question: is there a particular reason you made this a spreadable anomaly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Significant value and interest has been added to this SCiP, especially with the addition of the store manifesting within a drawer when the criteria are met.

+1 for improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh until the interview log. That put me over the top.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for finding those typos!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Review enclosed
- Thank you! Please come again!

I think "as this cause SCP-1940" should be "as this causes SCP-1940" or "as this will cause SCP-1940".

If the room in question is too small to fit these, its inner contents will appear to shrink to smaller proportions. However, this only affects SCP-1940-1, the counter and cash register, and the various shelves and displays holding products: the previous contents of the room do not change in size.

First things first, I think you want a semicolon, not a colon. Secondly, I'd probably refer to "If the room in question is too small to fit these items" but that may be personal taste.

Instances of SCP-1940-1 are of either gender

I have no idea where the Foundation is on the gender debate, but if you want to play it safe, that could be "any gender" to indicate that they might be trans/herm/neuter/etc

SCP-1940-1 can be killed, causing another instance of SCP-1940-1 to materialize when not under observation, and will try to reason with its attacker.

"and will" should probably be "which will"

An instance of SCP-1940 will cease to exist after all products of SCP-1940 are purchased.

Suggest "products within SCP-1940" or maybe "products offered for sale within SCP-1940" to indicate that they are the items that were reclassified by the SCP rather than ones created by it? Next sentence, again, you probably want a semicolon.

Attempts of surveillance to observe how SCP-1940 develops has been met with failure, due to all surveillance equipment being taken as products in SCP-1940. Recorded data show SCP-1940-1 locating any equipment and shutting then off before converting them into SCP-1940-2, though it will deny doing so upon interview.

"has been met" should be "have met". "shutting then off" should be "shutting them off"

I agree with the prior commentary on your thread that storing used condoms is very bizarre behavior. If you want to imply Agent Blackbox has been up to some infidelity, maybe change it to "used panties" or, more blatantly, "used underwear belonging to his mistress or wife"?

Overall, you have a few typos, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a step in the right direction, but it's not quite absurd enough for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roget gave some advice in improving this, and I changed the interview log to approach this humorously.
I apologize to those who have gotten sick of reading this.
